\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item \textit{Notes.} Net coefficient equals the M3A baseline $\hat{\beta}_{\text{HHI}}=-0.086$ plus the sector-specific interaction term. Mean and SD of HHI are computed within sector across all sample months. $\Delta RR$ per $+1\sigma$ HHI gives the predicted change in the one-month-ahead 25-delta risk reversal for a one-standard-deviation increase in sector HHI, holding all controls and fixed effects constant. Decimal units convert to basis points by multiplication by $10{,}000$ (a 25-delta risk reversal of $0.01$ corresponds to a $100$ bp put-call IV gap). Significance markers refer to the sector \emph{interaction} term: $^{*}\,p<0.10$, $^{**}\,p<0.05$, $^{***}\,p<0.01$ under entity-clustered standard errors. Sectors are sorted by absolute basis-point impact.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
